workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml

This workflow processes BMAD-ANNO annotations by dispatching them according to their intent field. It is the ACTION counterpart to scan-feedback (which only READS). The intent field maps 1:1 to BMAD correct-course severity levels.
LIFECYCLE AWARENESS: Each annotation carries a story_status field (from scan_feedback.py). The same intent has DIFFERENT semantics depending on whether the Story has been implemented: - PRE-IMPLEMENTATION (ready-for-dev): Annotations align intent before code is written. Safe to edit AC directly. - POST-IMPLEMENTATION (in-progress/review): Code already exists. AC changes risk drift. Post-impl change_ac is auto-escalated to moderate (correct-course discussion).

step.action: 1. Read the intent field: minor, moderate, or major
2. Read the scope field: story (default) or prd-section
3. Read the action field: change_ac, fix_bug, add_dev_note, rework_interaction, lock_decision, annotate_fr, erratum, upgrade_epic, question
4. Read the story_status field (provided by scan_feedback.py)
Rule A: If scope=story AND action=change_ac AND story_status in [in-progress, review]:
→ ESCALATE intent to moderate (even if user wrote minor)
→ Print: "⚠ AC 变更发生在代码已实现之后 — 自动升级为 moderate，走 correct-course 讨论"
→ Reason: Changing AC after code is written risks AC-code drift. Must discuss first.
Rule B: If scope=story AND action=rework_interaction AND intent=minor:
→ Print: "⚠ 交互重做不是小改 — 自动升级为 moderate"
6. Route to the appropriate step based on FINAL intent + scope + action:
- scope:prd-section + action:annotate_fr → Step 2f (Story draft from PRD)
PRECONDITION: story_status must be ready-for-dev (enforced by Step 1 escalation)
Replace the specified AC text with the new content from changes[]
1. Append entry to "### Deviation Notes": "[PRE-IMPL] {timestamp} ANNO-{id}: AC aligned before dev"

step.goal: Minor: Bug fix task (any lifecycle phase)
step.action: This step does NOT modify Acceptance Criteria — the AC was correct, the code had a defect.
1. Add bug reference to Dev Notes: "Bug Fix: {bug_id} — {reason}"
2. Create fix task in Tasks section: "- [ ] Fix {bug_id}: {description} (AC unchanged)"
3. If bug_id provided, link to /bmad-lb trace for full context
3. Remind user: after fix is implemented, re-run BMAD code-review (CR) to validate

step.goal: Minor: Add dev note (any lifecycle phase)
step.action: Append content to Dev Notes section
A. User explicitly wrote intent:moderate (feature direction needs rethinking)
B. System escalated from minor change_ac because story is post-implementation
In both cases, the flow is: DISCUSS FIRST, then decide what to change.
Prepare change description from the annotation's reason and details fields
Invoke the BMAD native correct-course workflow:
- Pass the change description as the triggering issue
- correct-course will generate a Sprint Change Proposal
2. Link the SCP to the originating annotation ID in Deviation Notes
Same as Step 2b but with additional escalation flag:
2. Add "ESCALATION: Route to PM/Architect for review" header to the SCP
4. Output warning to user: "This change may affect multiple Epics. Review the SCP before proceeding."
Open {prd_decisions} and update the corresponding decision line:
Include: original PRD text reference, user correction, reason, date
Based on the target_fr field, generate a Story draft file
Use the create-story-ext template but mark status as "draft-pending-review"
Extract the question from the annotation
Follow the OpenSpec decision protocol from .claude/rules/openspec-decision-protocol.md
Generate the copy-to-deep-research block for ChatGPT Deep Research
After all annotations are processed, output summary:
- How many annotations processed by category (with lifecycle phase: pre/post-impl)
- Remaining unprocessed annotations (if user selected partial processing)
